prompt: Create a cinematic, moody film-still portrait in a 16:9 widescreen frame of an elderly man with gray hair and a short beard sitting indoors beside a cold, grimy window on the left. The composition is a tight close-up with the man occupying the center and right side, his head partly cropped at the top, one ear and strands of gray hair visible on the right, and a worn tweed or wool coat over a dark shirt visible near the bottom. Place a large opaque square censor block over the center of his face, covering most facial features from forehead to lower face; the block should be a dark brown-to-black subtle gradient, perfectly rectangular, and centered slightly above the middle of the image. Use dim natural window light: cool greenish-blue daylight spilling from the left window, while the room on the right falls into deep shadow with warm, muted brown tones. The background should feel like an old interior, with a weathered window frame and dirty glass on the left and a blurred framed picture or wall detail on the right. Style it as gritty analog cinema, realistic live-action, shallow depth of field, soft film grain, muted desaturated colors, dramatic low-key lighting, subtle haze, and a tense introspective atmosphere. Avoid text, logos, extra people, and bright modern lighting.
